whatsapp.opener: Hello! I found Le Piaje online and would like some information about a stay.

hero.sub_title: Where the welcome is as genuine as the land's flavors
hero.main_text: A family farm in Tuscia where warm hospitality, honest food, and unspoiled nature come together.

reviews.name: Adriano
reviews.headline: The house is wonderful and the view is breathtaking.
reviews.quote: Just a few minutes from the lake, it guarantees incredible convenience. And at night, you can see the stars really well. The house is well maintained and beautiful. Giuliana was very kind. I highly recommend it for those who want to have a relaxing and peaceful vacation. I will be back.

reviews.name: Alba
reviews.headline: Fantastic place!!!!
reviews.quote: The owner is suuuuuper friendly. There's free breakfast and some parking spots in the property. The table and chairs in the courtyard create a very cozy space.

reviews.name: Aneliya
reviews.headline: It was perfect
reviews.quote: Everything was perfect. The host was very friendly, welcoming, and helpful throughout our stay. The accommodation was clean, comfortable, and had everything we needed. The location was excellent, just a short walk from the city centre, making it easy to explore the area. We really enjoyed our stay.

reviews.name: Tamuna
reviews.headline: I would love to go back!
reviews.quote: Everything was perfect. First of all I arrived by train it was late no buses at station and the host came by car and took me at home for free, the place was really clean, comfortable with sweet family vibe. Cute garden, good breakfast… I booked for one night and I stayed 2 and I would love to stay there even for a whole week.

reviews.name: Magnus
reviews.headline: This is it, you cant find a better deal in this area.
reviews.quote: A true find in this otherwise very expensive area. Close to town, about 10min walk or very short ride. Hosts are quite amazing and ammenities perfect. There is even a simple breakfast awailable, coffee and juice. With a car, its a great place to chill out after exploring the lake and areas around. And Bolena Lake and its lakeside towns is absolutely gorgeous, without being run over by the crowds. A true find.

reviews.name: Albert
reviews.headline: I really liked everything.
reviews.quote: A beautiful, cozy, huge house, a large property, you can walk among the vineyards and olive trees. Lake Bolsena is nearby, with wonderful free beaches and clear water. We recommend it without reservation.

reviews.name: Darlene
reviews.headline: This house is a hidden gem.
reviews.quote: When looking at the pictures on the listing it doesn't do it justice. We enjoyed the beautiful views and the tranquility of the farm we were overlooking. The house is as described on the listing. We used the kitchen as it was fully stocked and enjoyed eating outside every day overlooking the tremendous view. The location of the property is midway between the lake and Montefiascone.

reviews.name: Claire
reviews.headline: This is our favorite bnb we have ever stayed in!
reviews.quote: A beautiful spacious house in the country, huge garden, very private, and surrounded by olive groves. We gathered fruit and vegetables from the many plants in the garden and enjoyed relaxing on the portico in the shade. The kitchen was large and has everything you need to prepare meals, also a great coffee machine and coffee pods provided! The air-conditioning in every room was great as it's so hot this summer. The location was great too, 5 mins to Montefiascone which had many restaurants and shops, and also only a few minutes to the lake that has many little beaches for swimming. Just awesome thank you!!!

contact_form.title: Contact Us
contact_form.main_text: Have a question about our farm, accommodations, or homemade products? Send us a message and we will get back to you as soon as possible.

our_mission.title: Our Mission
our_mission.main_text: At Le Piaje we combine working agriculture with warm hospitality. We invite guests to experience our land through stays in the countryside, pilgrim hospitality on the Via Francigena, and the authentic tastes of our farm.
our_mission.bullet_point_one: Preserve and share the natural and cultural heritage of Montefiascone and Lake Bolsena
our_mission.bullet_point_two: Offer genuine agriturismo stays rooted in our family farm
our_mission.bullet_point_three: Welcome pilgrims and travelers with simple, honest hospitality
our_mission.bullet_point_four: Produce and share homemade food from our garden, vineyard, and olive groves

experiences_page.hero_subtitle: Discover the farm, the land, and the traditions of Tuscia
experiences_page.farm_tours_text: Walk among our vineyards, olive groves, and pomegranate trees. When the season allows, pick organic produce from the garden and learn about life on a working farm in the Pearl Valley near Lake Bolsena.
experiences_page.pilgrim_text: Al Centesimo Chilometro — Ristoro del Pellegrino — welcomes walkers on the Via Francigena with dormitory accommodation, a shared kitchen, and a peaceful chapel.
experiences_page.tasting_text: Taste the authentic flavors of our land: seasonal produce, olive oil, wine, and farm products available from our shop or to enjoy during your stay.
experiences_page.tourism_text: From Orvieto and Civita di Bagnoregio to Bomarzo and the shores of Lake Bolsena, Le Piaje is the perfect base for discovering central Italy's hidden countryside.

payment.success_message: Thank you for your payment. Your booking has been confirmed!
payment.email_sent: A confirmation email has been sent to your inbox.

villa_perlata.description:
The accommodation is 2 km from the shores of Lake Bolsena and close to many country trails. You'll love it for its views, vast outdoor spaces, atmosphere and privacy. My place is good for couples and families (with kids).

Located in the so-called "Valle Perlata", you will have the opportunity to immerse yourself in nature, crossing numerous paths and walking to the lake without being disturbed by the noise of traffic. The accommodation is framed by the farm's fields, where fruit, olives and especially grapes are produced.

In addition to the garden, you can walk among the pomegranate and olive groves in front of the house and among the rows of the two vineyards to the left of it, where you can enjoy a magnificent view. If the season allows, you can harvest the produce from the garden and the organically grown fruit trees.

The rooms are located on the second floor. You'll find both bed linens and towels. Utilities are included in the price.

centesimo.description:
Al Centesimo Chilometro — Ristoro del Pellegrino sits in Montefiascone, one of the historic stopping points on the Via Francigena, the great medieval pilgrimage route from Canterbury to Rome. For centuries, walkers crossed the hills of Tuscia on foot, seeking food, rest, and shelter before the final stretch toward the Eternal City.

That tradition of welcome continues here today. We offer simple, honest hospitality for pilgrims and travellers: dormitory beds for men and women, a garden to pause in, free private parking, and a shared lounge where stories from the road are exchanged over a meal or a glass of wine.

Whether you are walking the Francigena or exploring Lake Bolsena and the vineyards of Montefiascone, our hostel is a practical, genuine base in the heart of Tuscia.
centesimo.francigena_link_label: Learn more about the Via Francigena

villa_perlata.name: La Villa Perlata
villa_perlata.about: In the Pearl Valley, 2 km from Lake Bolsena. A farm stay surrounded by vineyards and olive groves — views, privacy, and country paths to the lake.

centesimo.name: Al Centesimo Chilometro
centesimo.about: Located in Montefiascone, Al Centesimo Chilometro — Ristoro del Pellegrino welcomes pilgrims and travellers with a garden, free private parking, and a shared lounge. A practical stop along the Via Francigena.

rocca_papi.name: Rocca dei Papi
rocca_papi.about: La Rocca dei Papi is the ancient fortress built by Innocent III at the end of the twelfth century. It stands 633 metres above sea level, guarding the village of Montefiascone from the top of the hill — a strategic vantage point over the surrounding countryside and Lake Bolsena.

isola_bisentina.name: Isola Bisentina
isola_bisentina.about: A private island in the heart of Lake Bolsena, one of Europe's largest volcanic lakes. Open to guided visits with historic churches, nature trails, and contemporary art — inhabited since Etruscan and Roman times.

civita_di_bagnoregio.name: Civita di Bagnoregio
civita_di_bagnoregio.about: Known as "the dying city", this Etruscan village perches on a tuff spur above the Valle dei Calanchi. Reachable only by a footbridge, it is one of Italy's most enchanting borghi — medieval lanes, timeless atmosphere, and breathtaking views.

villa_lante.name: Villa Lante
villa_lante.about: Renaissance villa and garden in Bagnaia, famed for its Mannerist terraces, fountains, and geometric parterres cascading down the hillside. A national museum and one of Italy's finest historic gardens.

sacro_bosco.name: Sacro Bosco
sacro_bosco.about: The Sacred Wood of Bomarzo — a 16th-century Mannerist garden of monstrous sculptures, temples, and inscriptions carved in volcanic rock. A surreal walk through myth, wonder, and Renaissance imagination.

rocca_monaldeschi.name: Rocca Monaldeschi della Cervara
rocca_monaldeschi.about: Medieval fortress on the shores of Lake Bolsena, overlooking the town from its rocky spur. Home to a museum tracing the history of the lake and its communities.

duomo_orvieto.name: Duomo di Orvieto
duomo_orvieto.about: Cathedral of Santa Maria Assunta — a Gothic-Romanesque masterpiece famed for its dazzling façade, Signorelli frescoes, and commanding hilltop perch above the Umbrian countryside.
